title: Configure a PodDisruptionBudget
description:
## PodDisruptionBudget

A **PodDisruptionBudget (PDB)** limits the number of pods of a replicated application that are down simultaneously during voluntary disruptions (node drains, cluster upgrades). It is a critical CKA topic.

**Your task:**

A Deployment named `pdb-app` with **3 replicas** is already running. Create a **PodDisruptionBudget** named `web-pdb` in the `default` namespace that:
- Applies to pods with the label `app=pdb-app`
- Ensures **at least 2 pods** are always available (`minAvailable: 2`)

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get pdb web-pdb
```

hints.title: PodDisruptionBudget manifest
hints.body: Use `spec.minAvailable` (integer or percentage) and `spec.selector.matchLabels` to target the right pods.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: web-pdb
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: pdb-app
EOF

hints.title: Check the PDB status
hints.body: After creating the PDB, check `kubectl get pdb web-pdb` — the ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS column shows how many pods can be disrupted at once.
